texto: Instead of a book, what if we're actually writing (or not writing) in the margins of our lives? What if our lives are books? What is the sign of our presence? Are we pressing into the margins our interpretations and questions? Are we circling offending verbs and drawing furious arrows to the margin where we scrawl "irony," "frustration," "voiceless," "unfair!" Or do we simply turn the pages, passively receiving what's given, furiously disagreeing but remaining silent about it?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Patti Digh que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “Instead of a book, what if we're actually writing (or not writing) in the margins of our lives? W…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre vida, reading, writing, com foco em termino e reading.
